name: Bench Press (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on the bench with your eyes directly under the bar, feet planted firmly on the floor 2. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width, keeping your wrists stacked over your elbows throughout 3. Unrack the bar and position it over your mid-chest 4. Lower the bar in a controlled manner to your mid-chest, allowing your elbows to track at roughly 45-75 degrees from your torso 5. Press the bar forcefully back to the starting position until your arms are fully extended

name: Bench Press (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on the bench with dumbbells resting on your thighs, then kick them up as you lie back, positioning them at chest level 2. Plant your feet firmly on the floor and keep your wrists stacked over your elbows throughout the movement 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until they are roughly in line with your chest, elbows tracking at 45-75 degrees from your torso 4. Press the dumbbells forcefully back up and slightly inward until your arms are fully extended

name: Bench Press (Smith)
instruction: 1. Position the bench so the bar will travel over your mid-chest, then lie back with feet planted firmly on the floor 2. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Unrack by rotating the bar and lower it in a controlled manner to your mid-chest 4. Press forcefully back to the starting position until your arms are fully extended, then re-rack by rotating the bar

name: Incline Bench Press (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with eyes under the bar and feet planted firmly on the floor 2. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Unrack the bar and position it over your upper chest 4. Lower the bar in a controlled manner to your upper chest, keeping elbows at roughly 45-75 degrees from your torso 5. Press forcefully back to the starting position until your arms are fully extended

name: Incline Bench Press (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline, sit with dumbbells on your thighs, then kick them up as you lie back to chest level 2. Keep your wrists stacked over your elbows throughout the movement 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner to upper chest level, elbows tracking at 45-75 degrees from your torso 4. Press the dumbbells forcefully back up until your arms are fully extended

name: Incline Bench Press (Smith)
instruction: 1. Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline and position it so the bar will travel over your upper chest 2. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Unrack by rotating the bar and lower it in a controlled manner to your upper chest 4. Press forcefully to the starting position until your arms are fully extended, then re-rack

name: Decline Bench Press (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Secure your feet under the pads and lie back on the decline bench with eyes under the bar 2. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Unrack the bar and position it over your lower chest 4. Lower the bar in a controlled manner to your lower chest with elbows tracking at 45-75 degrees 5. Press forcefully back to the starting position until your arms are fully extended

name: Decline Bench Press (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Secure your feet under the pads, sit with dumbbells on your thighs, and carefully lie back on the decline bench 2. Position the dumbbells at lower chest level with your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner to lower chest level, elbows tracking at 45-75 degrees 4. Press forcefully back up until your arms are fully extended

name: Chest Press (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles are at mid-chest height and your back is fully supported against the pad 2. Grip the handles with your wrists stacked over your elbows and feet planted firmly on the floor 3. Press the handles forward forcefully until your arms are nearly fully extended 4. Return the handles in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Incline Chest Press (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles are at upper-chest height and your back is fully against the pad 2. Grip the handles with your wrists stacked over your elbows and feet planted on the floor 3. Press the handles upward and forward forcefully until your arms are nearly fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Chest Press (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles align with your mid-chest, back fully against the pad 2. Grip the handles with your wrists stacked over your elbows and feet planted firmly 3. Press the handles forward forcefully until your arms are nearly fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, maintaining constant tension
alias: ["plate loaded chest press", "hammer strength chest press"]

name: Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended above your chest with a slight bend in the elbows 2. Lower the dumbbells in a wide arc in a controlled manner until you feel a comfortable stretch across your chest 3. Squeeze your pecs and bring the dumbbells back up in the same arc to the starting position

name: Incline Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with a dumbbell in each hand extended above your upper chest with a slight elbow bend 2. Lower the dumbbells in a wide arc in a controlled manner until you feel a comfortable stretch across your upper chest 3. Squeeze your pecs and bring the dumbbells back up in the same arc to the starting position

name: Standing Chest Fly (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set both pulleys to chest height, stand in the center of the cable station and grip a handle in each hand with a slight elbow bend 2. Stagger your stance slightly for stability and lean very slightly forward at the hips 3. Bring both handles together in a wide arc in front of your chest, squeezing your pecs at the point of peak contraction 4. Return both handles in a controlled manner in the same arc to the starting position

name: Low-to-High Chest Fly (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set both pulleys to the lowest position, grip a handle in each hand, and stand centered with a slight forward hip hinge 2. With a slight bend in your elbows, bring the handles upward and together in an arc toward upper chest height, squeezing your pecs at the top 3. Return both handles in a controlled manner back down along the same arc to the starting position
alias: ["low cable fly", "low to high fly", "cable crossover low"]

name: High-to-Low Chest Fly (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set both pulleys to the highest position, grip a handle in each hand, and stand centered with a slight forward lean 2. With a slight bend in your elbows, bring the handles downward and together in an arc toward lower chest height, squeezing your pecs at the bottom 3. Return both handles in a controlled manner back up along the same arc to the starting position
alias: ["high cable fly", "high to low fly", "cable crossover high"]

name: Pec Deck (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles or arm pads are at chest height and your back is fully supported 2. Place your forearms or grip the handles with elbows at chest height 3. Bring the pads or handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest, squeezing your pecs at the peak 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Push-Up (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Place your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, body forming a straight line from head to heels 2. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a rigid body position throughout 3. Lower your chest to the floor in a controlled manner, elbows tracking at roughly 45-75 degrees from your torso 4. Press forcefully back up to the starting position until your arms are fully extended

name: Chest Dips (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Grip parallel bars and support yourself with arms extended, then lean your torso forward at roughly 30-45 degrees 2. Lower yourself in a controlled manner until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch across your chest 3. Press forcefully back up to the starting position, maintaining the forward lean throughout

name: Chest Dips (Assisted)
instruction: 1. Adjust the weight assistance and kneel or stand on the platform, grip parallel bars and lean your torso forward at roughly 30-45 degrees 2. Lower yourself in a controlled manner until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor 3. Press forcefully back up to the starting position, maintaining the forward lean throughout

name: Seated Shoulder Press (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Set the bar at upper-chest height in a rack, grip it slightly wider than shoulder-width with wrists stacked over your elbows 2. Unrack and position the bar at the top of your chest in front of your head, bracing your core 3. Press the bar forcefully overhead until your arms are fully extended 4. Lower the bar in a controlled manner back to the starting position at upper chest level

name: Seated Shoulder Press (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on an upright bench and hold dumbbells at shoulder height with your wrists stacked over your elbows, palms facing forward 2. Press the dumbbells forcefully overhead until your arms are fully extended 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to shoulder height

name: Seated Shoulder Press (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles are at shoulder height and your back is supported against the pad 2. Grip the handles with your wrists stacked over your elbows and feet planted on the floor 3. Press the handles overhead forcefully until your arms are nearly fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down
alias: ["machine shoulder press", "seated shoulder press", "machine overhead press"]

name: Seated Shoulder Press (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the handles align with your shoulders, back fully against the pad 2. Grip the handles with your wrists stacked over your elbows and feet planted 3. Press forcefully overhead until your arms are nearly fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, maintaining constant tension
alias: ["plate loaded shoulder press", "hammer strength shoulder press"]

name: Seated Shoulder Press (Smith)
instruction: 1. Position the bench under the bar set at upper-chest height, grip slightly wider than shoulder-width with wrists stacked over elbows 2. Unrack by rotating the bar, bracing your core 3. Press the bar forcefully overhead until your arms are fully extended 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then re-rack
alias: ["smith shoulder press", "smith military press", "smith overhead press"]

name: Arnold Press (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit upright on a bench holding dumbbells at chin height with palms facing toward you and elbows close together 2. Simultaneously rotate your palms outward to face forward as you press the dumbbells overhead 3. Continue pressing until arms are fully extended overhead 4. Return in a controlled manner, rotating the palms back toward you as you lower to the starting position

name: Lateral Raise (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with dumbbells at your sides, a slight bend in your elbows, and your torso upright 2. Raise the dumbbells outward and upward in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor, leading with the backs of your hands 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to your sides

name: Lateral Raise (Cable)
instruction: 1. Stand beside the cable stack with the pulley set at the lowest position, grip the handle with the arm farthest from the machine 2. Maintain a slight bend in your elbow and a stable upright posture 3. Raise the handle outward and upward in a controlled arc until your arm is roughly parallel to the floor 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Lateral Raise (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Stand beside the cable stack with the pulley at the lowest position, grip the handle with the arm farthest from the machine 2. Maintain a slight elbow bend and a stable upright posture 3. Raise the handle outward and upward in a controlled arc until your arm is roughly parallel to the floor 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position
alias: ["single arm lateral raise", "one arm cable lateral raise"]

name: Lateral Raise (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat and arm pads so your elbows rest comfortably at your sides at roughly pad height, back supported 2. Drive your elbows outward and upward against the pads in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Lateral Raise (Band)
instruction: 1. Stand on the center of the band with feet shoulder-width apart, holding one end in each hand at your sides 2. Maintain a slight elbow bend and stable upright posture 3. Raise your arms outward and upward in a controlled arc until they are roughly parallel to the floor 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to your sides

name: Front Raise (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs with a slight elbow bend, palms facing your body 2. Raise one or both dumbbells forward and upward in a controlled arc to approximately shoulder height 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Front Raise (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with an overhand grip on the bar, hands shoulder-width apart, bar resting at thigh level 2. Maintain a slight elbow bend and stable upright posture 3. Raise the bar forward and upward in a controlled arc to approximately shoulder height 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Front Raise (Cable)
instruction: 1. Stand facing away from the cable stack with the pulley at the lowest position, grip the handle with an overhand grip 2. Maintain a slight elbow bend and stable upright posture 3. Raise the handle forward and upward in a controlled arc to approximately shoulder height 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Rear Delt Fly (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, holding dumbbells below your chest with a slight elbow bend 2. Raise the dumbbells outward and upward in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor, leading with the backs of your hands 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position
alias: ["reverse fly", "rear delt fly", "bent over rear fly"]

name: Rear Delt Fly (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set both pulleys to chest height and cross the cables so you grab the left pulley with your right hand and vice versa 2. Stand centered with a slight forward torso lean and a slight elbow bend in each arm 3. Pull the handles outward and backward in a controlled arc until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Rear Delt Fly (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to chest height and stand beside the cable stack, gripping the handle with the opposite hand 2. Maintain a slight forward torso lean and a slight elbow bend 3. Pull the handle outward and backward in a controlled arc until your arm is roughly parallel to the floor 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position
alias: ["single arm rear delt fly", "one arm cable rear fly"]

name: Rear Delt Fly (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat and grip or arm pads so your arms are at chest height with a slight elbow bend, chest against the pad 2. Drive your arms outward and backward in a controlled arc until they are roughly parallel to the floor 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down
alias: ["reverse pec deck", "machine rear delt fly", "machine reverse fly"]

name: Face Pull (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley at or slightly above head height and attach a rope handle, gripping one end in each hand with palms facing inward 2. Step back to create tension in the cable, bracing your core 3. Pull the rope toward your face, driving your elbows up and back while rotating your hands so your palms face forward at the end position 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Upright Row (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly narrower than shoulder-width, bar at thigh level 2. Pull the bar upward along your body, leading with your elbows until the bar reaches upper-chest or chin height 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Upright Row (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and grip the bar or handles with an overhand grip, hands slightly narrower than shoulder-width 2. Stand upright with the cable taut 3. Pull the handle upward along your body, leading with your elbows until it reaches upper-chest height 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Upright Row (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart 2. Pull the dumbbells upward along your body, leading with your elbows until they reach upper-chest height 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Shrug (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the barbell with an overhand grip at hip level, arms fully extended 2. Elevate your shoulders straight upward as high as possible without rotating them 3. Hold briefly at the top, then lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Shrug (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with arms fully extended 2. Elevate your shoulders straight upward as high as possible without rotating them 3. Hold briefly at the top, then lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Shrug (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the machine to hold the handles at hip or thigh level with arms fully extended, back upright 2. Elevate your shoulders straight upward as high as possible without rolling them 3. Hold briefly at the top, then lower in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Shrug (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulleys to the lowest position and grip the handles or bar at hip level with arms fully extended 2. Elevate your shoulders straight upward as high as possible 3. Hold briefly at the top, then lower in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Shrug (Smith)
instruction: 1. Position the Smith bar at hip level, grip it with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width 2. Unrack and stand upright with arms fully extended 3. Elevate your shoulders straight upward as high as possible 4. Hold briefly at the top, then lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Pull-Up (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width and hang with arms fully extended 2. Brace your core and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips until your chin clears the bar 3. Lower yourself in a controlled manner back to a fully extended hang

name: Pull-Up (Assisted)
instruction: 1. Adjust the counterweight assistance and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, kneeling or standing on the platform 2. Brace your core and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips until your chin clears the bar 3. Lower yourself in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Chin-Up (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Grip the bar with an underhand grip at approximately shoulder-width and hang with arms fully extended 2. Brace your core and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips until your chin clears the bar 3. Lower yourself in a controlled manner back to a fully extended hang

name: Chin-Up (Assisted)
instruction: 1. Adjust the counterweight and grip the bar with an underhand grip at approximately shoulder-width, kneeling or standing on the platform 2. Brace your core and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips until your chin clears the bar 3. Lower yourself in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Lat Pulldown (Cable)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad to secure your legs, grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width 2. Sit tall and allow your arms to fully extend overhead to start 3. Drive your elbows down toward your hips to pull the bar to upper-chest height 4. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up — stop before the lats lose leverage — and repeat

name: Lat Pulldown (Cable, Wide Grip)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad, grip the bar at the wide outer sections with an overhand grip 2. Sit tall and allow your arms to fully extend overhead 3. Drive your elbows down toward your hips to pull the bar to upper-chest height 4. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating

name: Lat Pulldown (Cable, Underhand)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad, grip the bar with an underhand grip at approximately shoulder-width 2. Sit tall and allow your arms to fully extend overhead 3. Drive your elbows down toward your hips to pull the bar to upper-chest height 4. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating

name: Lat Pulldown (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad and seat, grip the handles with arms extended overhead 2. Sit tall and drive your elbows down toward your hips to pull the handles to upper-chest height 3. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating

name: Lat Pulldown (Machine, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat and knee pad, grip one handle with one hand, arm extended overhead 2. Sit tall and drive your elbow down toward your hip to pull the handle to upper-chest height 3. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm machine lat pulldown", "one arm machine lat pulldown"]

name: Lat Pulldown (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad and attach a single handle to the cable, grip it with one hand overhead 2. Sit tall and drive your elbow down toward your hip to pull the handle to upper-chest height 3. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm cable lat pulldown", "one arm cable lat pulldown"]

name: Lat Pulldown (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Adjust the knee pad and grip the handles with an overhand grip, arms extended overhead 2. Sit tall and drive your elbows down toward your hips to pull the bar or handles to upper-chest height 3. Return in a controlled manner to approximately halfway up before repeating

name: Lat Pullover (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so the arm pads align with your elbows, sit tall and grip the handles with arms extended overhead 2. Drive your elbows downward in an arc toward your hips, feeling a stretch in your lats at the top 3. Return in a controlled manner along the same arc to the overhead starting position

name: Lat Pullover (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Lie perpendicular across a bench with your upper back supported, holding a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms nearly extended 2. Lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc over and behind your head until you feel a stretch in your lats 3. Drive the dumbbell back up in the same arc to the starting position above your chest

name: Seated Lat Row (Cable)
instruction: 1. Sit at the cable row station with feet on the footpads, knees slightly bent, grip the handle with both hands and arms extended 2. Sit tall with a neutral spine, keeping your wrists stacked over your elbows 3. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to pull the handle to your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position with arms fully extended

name: Seated Upper Back Row (Cable)
instruction: 1. Sit at the cable row station, grip the wide bar or handles with arms extended at roughly upper-chest height, wrists stacked over elbows 2. Sit tall with a neutral spine 3. Drive your elbows back and slightly outward to pull the handle toward your upper abdomen or lower chest 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Incline Upper Back Row (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Lie face down on an incline bench set at about 30-45 degrees, holding dumbbells below you with arms extended, wrists stacked over elbows 2. Drive your elbows back and slightly outward, pulling the dumbbells toward your upper abdomen 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Chest-Supported Lat Row (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the chest pad and seat so your chest rests fully against the pad, grip the handles with wrists stacked over elbows and arms extended 2. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to pull the handles to your lower abdomen 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down
alias: ["chest supported row machine", "machine chest supported row"]

name: Chest-Supported Upper Back Row (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the chest pad and seat with your chest fully supported, grip handles at upper-chest level with wrists stacked over elbows 2. Drive your elbows back and slightly outward to pull the handles toward your upper abdomen 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Chest-Supported Lat Row (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Adjust the chest pad so your chest is fully supported, grip handles with arms extended and wrists stacked over elbows 2. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to pull the handles toward your lower abdomen 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Chest-Supported Upper Back Row (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Adjust the chest pad so your chest is fully supported, grip handles at upper-chest level with wrists stacked over elbows 2. Drive your elbows back and slightly outward to pull toward your upper abdomen 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Bent-Over Row (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, grip the bar with an overhand grip and arms extended, wrists stacked over elbows 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine throughout 3. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to row the bar to your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position with arms fully extended

name: Bent-Over Row (Barbell, Underhand)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, hinge forward until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, grip the bar with an underhand grip and arms extended, wrists stacked over elbows 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine 3. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to row the bar to your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position
alias: ["underhand barbell row", "supinated barbell row", "reverse grip row"]

name: Bent-Over Row (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, hinge forward until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, hold dumbbells below you with arms extended and wrists stacked over elbows 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine 3. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to row the dumbbells to your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Bent-Over Row (Dumbbell, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Place one hand and same-side knee on a bench for support, holding a dumbbell in the other hand below you with arm extended and wrist stacked over elbow 2. Maintain a neutral spine and stable upper body 3. Drive your elbow back toward your hip to row the dumbbell to your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm row", "one arm row", "single arm dumbbell row"]

name: T-Bar Lat Row (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Straddle the T-bar with feet hip-width apart, hinge forward until your torso is at roughly 45 degrees, grip the handles with arms extended and wrists stacked over elbows 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine 3. Drive your elbows back toward your hips to row the handles toward your lower abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: T-Bar Upper Back Row (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Straddle the T-bar, hinge forward at about 45 degrees, grip the handles with arms extended and wrists stacked over elbows 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine 3. Drive your elbows back and slightly outward to row toward your upper abdomen 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position
alias: ["t bar upper back row", "t-bar upper row"]

name: Pendlay Row (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and the bar over your mid-foot, hinge forward until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor and grip the bar just outside shoulder-width 2. Begin each rep from a dead stop on the floor with the bar touching down 3. Drive your elbows back forcefully to row the bar to your lower abdomen 4. Lower the bar in a controlled manner back to the floor completely before the next rep

name: Deadlift (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and the bar over your mid-foot, grip just outside your legs with a double overhand or mixed grip 2. Lower your hips until your shins contact the bar, chest up, neutral spine 3. Drive your feet into the floor and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift the bar 4. Lock out your hips fully at the top, then return the bar in a controlled manner by hinging at the hips first, then bending your knees once the bar passes them

name: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the bar at hip level with an overhand grip, feet hip-width apart 2. Push your hips back and hinge forward, lowering the bar along your legs with a slight knee bend and a neutral spine until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings 3. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position

name: Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells at hip level with arms extended, feet hip-width apart 2. Push your hips back and hinge forward, lowering the dumbbells along your legs with a slight knee bend and neutral spine until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings 3. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position

name: Romanian Deadlift (Dumbbell, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand, the free leg extended behind you for balance 2. Hinge forward at the hip of the standing leg, lowering the dumbbell toward the floor until you feel a strong hamstring stretch 3. Drive your hip forward to return to an upright position, then switch sides

name: Romanian Deadlift (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and grip the handle or straight bar with both hands, step back to create tension 2. Hinge forward at the hips with a slight knee bend and neutral spine, lowering until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings 3. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position

name: Sumo Deadlift (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, toes turned out, bar over your mid-foot, grip inside your legs with arms extended 2. Lower your hips while keeping your chest up and spine neutral, shins near vertical 3. Drive your feet outward into the floor and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift the bar 4. Lock out your hips at the top, then return in a controlled manner by reversing the movement

name: Sumo Deadlift (Smith)
instruction: 1. Position the Smith bar and set your stance wide with toes turned out, grip the bar inside your legs 2. Lower your hips while keeping your chest up and spine neutral 3. Drive your feet outward into the floor and extend your hips and knees to lift 4. Lock out at the top then return in a controlled manner, re-racking by rotating the bar

name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the bar at hip level with an overhand grip, keeping your legs nearly straight throughout 2. Hinge at the hips and lower the bar along your legs until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings, allowing only a minimal knee bend 3. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position

name: Trap Bar Deadlift (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand inside the trap bar with feet hip-width apart, grip the handles at your sides 2. Lower your hips until your shins are roughly vertical, chest up, neutral spine 3. Drive your feet into the floor and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift 4. Lock out your hips at the top, then return in a controlled manner by reversing the movement

name: Good Morning (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the bar across your upper traps as in a squat, stand with feet hip-width apart 2. Maintain a slight knee bend and a braced, neutral spine throughout 3. Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings 4. Drive your hips forward to return to the upright starting position

name: Back Extension (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the machine so the pad rests across your upper thighs, hips at the pivot point, feet secured on the footpads 2. Hold your arms across your chest or behind your head, starting in a hinged position 3. Extend your hips to raise your torso until it is in line with your lower body 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Back Extension (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Position yourself on the back extension bench so the pad is across your upper thighs and hips are at the edge, feet secured 2. Hold your arms across your chest or behind your head, starting in a hinged position 3. Extend your hips to raise your torso until it aligns with your legs 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Hyperextension (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Position yourself on the hyperextension bench with the pad across your upper thighs and hips at the edge, feet secured, holding a plate to your chest 2. Start in a hinged position with your torso lowered 3. Extend your hips to raise your torso until it aligns with your legs 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Biceps Curl (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the barbell with an underhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended at your sides 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc until your forearms are vertical and peak contraction is reached 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Biceps Curl (Barbell, EZ)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the EZ curl bar with an underhand grip on the angled inner grips, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Biceps Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction, keeping wrists neutral 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Alternating Biceps Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding a dumbbell at one side with palm facing forward, arm fully extended 2. Keep your elbow pinned at your side throughout 3. Curl the dumbbell upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Hammer Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing your body, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl the dumbbells upward in a controlled arc with a neutral grip to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Biceps Curl (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and grip the straight bar or handle with an underhand grip, stand back to create tension 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides 3. Curl the bar or handle upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Biceps Curl (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and grip a single handle with an underhand grip, step back to create tension 2. Keep your elbow pinned at your side 3. Curl the handle upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm cable curl", "one arm cable curl"]

name: Biceps Curl (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so your elbows rest on the pad at chest height, grip the handles with an underhand grip and arms extended 2. Keep your upper arms flat against the pad throughout 3. Curl the handles upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Incline Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Set a bench to 45-60 degrees, lie back with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging fully extended below you with palms facing forward 2. Keep your elbows pointing down and fixed in place throughout 3. Curl the dumbbells upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Preacher Curl (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Sit at the preacher bench with upper arms resting flat on the angled pad, grip the barbell with an underhand grip 2. Keep your upper arms pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward full extension, stopping just short to keep tension

name: Preacher Curl (Barbell, EZ)
instruction: 1. Sit at the preacher bench with upper arms resting on the pad, grip the EZ bar on the inner angled grips with an underhand grip 2. Keep your upper arms pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward full extension

name: Preacher Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit at the preacher bench with one upper arm resting flat on the pad, hold a dumbbell with an underhand grip 2. Keep your upper arm pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the dumbbell upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward full extension, then switch sides

name: Preacher Curl (Dumbbell, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Sit at the preacher bench with one upper arm resting on the pad, grip a dumbbell with an underhand grip 2. Keep your upper arm pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the dumbbell upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward full extension, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm preacher curl", "one arm preacher curl"]

name: Preacher Hammer Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit at the preacher bench with one upper arm resting on the pad, grip a dumbbell with a neutral (hammer) grip 2. Keep your upper arm pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the dumbbell upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward full extension, then switch sides

name: Preacher Curl (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so your upper arms rest flat on the pad, grip the handles with an underhand grip and arms extended 2. Keep your upper arms pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the handles upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Preacher Curl (Machine, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so one upper arm rests flat on the pad, grip the single handle with an underhand grip 2. Keep your upper arm pressed against the pad throughout 3. Curl the handle upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm machine preacher curl", "one arm preacher machine"]

name: Concentration Curl (Dumbbell, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench with feet wide, hold a dumbbell in one hand and brace that elbow against your inner thigh with arm extended 2. Keep your elbow braced against your thigh throughout 3. Curl the dumbbell upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Spider Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Lie face down on an incline bench set at about 45 degrees, letting your arms hang fully extended below you with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward 2. Keep your upper arms perpendicular to the floor and pinned in place throughout 3. Curl the dumbbells upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Reverse Curl (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding the barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction, maintaining the overhand grip 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Reverse Curl (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position, grip the bar with an overhand grip and step back to create tension 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides 3. Curl the bar upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction with the overhand grip 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Reverse Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells with an overhand grip, arms fully extended at your sides 2. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout 3. Curl the dumbbells upward in a controlled arc to peak contraction, maintaining the overhand grip 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Zottman Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells with palms facing forward, arms fully extended 2. Curl the dumbbells upward in a controlled arc with an underhand grip to peak contraction 3. At the top, rotate your wrists so your palms face downward 4. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to the starting position with the overhand grip, then rotate palms forward to begin the next rep

name: Triceps Pushdown (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position and grip the straight bar with an overhand grip, elbows pinned at your sides 2. Keep your elbows fixed at your sides throughout 3. Push the bar downward in a controlled arc until your arms are fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, allowing the bar to rise until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor

name: Triceps Pushdown (Cable, Rope)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position and grip the rope ends with palms facing each other, elbows pinned at your sides 2. Keep your elbows fixed at your sides throughout 3. Push the rope downward until arms are fully extended, spreading the ends slightly apart at the bottom 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Incline Triceps Pushdown (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position, face away from the stack or position yourself on an incline bench, gripping the bar or rope overhead 2. Keep your elbows pointing forward and fixed in place 3. Extend your arms downward in a controlled arc to full extension 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Triceps Extension (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position and grip a single handle with one hand, elbow pinned at your side 2. Keep your elbow fixed throughout 3. Push the handle downward until your arm is fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides
alias: ["single arm tricep extension", "one arm cable tricep"]

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position, face away from the stack, and grip the rope or bar overhead with arms extended 2. Keep your elbows pointing forward and close together throughout 3. Lower the handle in a controlled arc behind your head until your elbows are fully bent 4. Extend your arms overhead forcefully to the starting position

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand or sit holding a dumbbell overhead with both hands, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pointing forward and close together throughout 3. Lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head until your elbows are fully bent 4. Extend your arms forcefully overhead to the starting position

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Barbell, EZ)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench holding the EZ bar overhead with an overhand grip, arms fully extended 2. Keep your elbows pointing forward and close together throughout 3. Lower the bar in a controlled arc behind your head until your elbows are fully bent 4. Extend your arms forcefully overhead to the starting position

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat and grip the overhead handle with arms extended, elbows pointing forward 2. Keep your elbows close together and pointing forward throughout 3. Lower the handle in a controlled arc behind your head until elbows are fully bent 4. Extend your arms forcefully to the starting position

name: Skull Crusher (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on a bench holding the barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width, arms extended over your chest 2. Keep your upper arms fixed perpendicular to the floor throughout 3. Lower the bar in a controlled arc toward your forehead or slightly behind your head 4. Extend your arms forcefully back to the starting position

name: Skull Crusher (Barbell, EZ)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on a bench holding the EZ bar with an overhand grip on the inner angled sections, arms extended over your chest 2. Keep your upper arms fixed perpendicular to the floor throughout 3. Lower the bar in a controlled arc toward your forehead or slightly behind your head 4. Extend your arms forcefully back to the starting position

name: Skull Crusher (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on a bench holding dumbbells overhead with arms extended, palms facing each other 2. Keep your upper arms fixed perpendicular to the floor throughout 3. Lower the dumbbells in a controlled arc toward the sides of your head 4. Extend your arms forcefully back to the starting position

name: Triceps Kickback (Cable, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position, hinge forward, grip the handle with one hand, elbow bent at 90 degrees and upper arm parallel to the floor 2. Keep your upper arm fixed in place throughout 3. Extend your forearm backward in a controlled arc until your arm is fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the 90-degree starting position, then switch sides

name: Triceps Extension (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so your elbows align with the machine pivot, arms bent at roughly 90 degrees, grip the handles 2. Keep your upper arms stationary throughout 3. Push the handles downward in a controlled arc until your arms are fully extended 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Triceps Dips (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Grip parallel bars and support yourself with arms extended, keeping your torso upright 2. Lower yourself in a controlled manner by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body, until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor 3. Press forcefully back up to the starting position

name: Triceps Dips (Assisted)
instruction: 1. Adjust the weight assistance, grip parallel bars and kneel or stand on the platform, keeping your torso upright 2. Lower yourself in a controlled manner by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body, until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor 3. Press forcefully back up to the starting position

name: Close-Grip Bench Press (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on the bench, grip the bar at roughly shoulder-width with wrists stacked over your elbows 2. Unrack the bar and position it over your lower chest, keeping your elbows close to your torso 3. Lower the bar in a controlled manner to your lower chest 4. Press forcefully back up until your arms are fully extended

name: Seated Wrist Curl (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench and rest your forearms on your thighs or the bench with palms facing up, holding the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand grip 2. Let your wrists extend downward to a comfortable stretch 3. Curl your wrists upward through their full range of motion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Seated Wrist Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench, rest your forearm on your thigh with palm facing up, holding a dumbbell 2. Let your wrist extend downward to a comfortable stretch 3. Curl your wrist upward through its full range of motion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Reverse Wrist Curl (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench and rest your forearms on your thighs with palms facing down, holding the barbell with an overhand grip 2. Let your wrists flex downward 3. Extend your wrists upward through their full range of motion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Reverse Wrist Curl (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on a bench, rest your forearm on your thigh with palm facing down, holding a dumbbell 2. Let your wrist flex downward 3. Extend your wrist upward through its full range of motion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Farmers Carry (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding a heavy dumbbell in each hand at your sides with arms extended and an upright posture 2. Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine 3. Walk forward at a steady controlled pace for the prescribed distance or time 4. Set the dumbbells down in a controlled manner at completion

name: Squat (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the bar across your upper traps, stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes turned out slightly 2. Brace your core and maintain an upright torso throughout 3. Descend in a controlled manner by pushing your knees outward in line with your toes until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor or deeper 4. Drive through your feet forcefully to return to the starting position

name: Squat (Smith, Low Stance)
instruction: 1. Position the Smith bar across your upper traps, set your feet slightly forward and shoulder-width apart, toes turned out slightly 2. Brace your core and unrack by rotating the bar 3. Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor, knees tracking in line with toes 4. Drive through your feet forcefully to return to the starting position
alias: ["smith squat", "smith machine squat, smith squat quads"]

name: Squat (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes turned out slightly, arms extended forward for balance 2. Brace your core and maintain an upright torso throughout 3. Descend in a controlled manner by pushing your knees out in line with your toes until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor 4. Drive through your feet forcefully to return to the starting position

name: Front Squat (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the bar across the front of your shoulders in a clean rack grip or cross-arm position, elbows raised high, feet shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out 2. Brace your core and keep your elbows elevated throughout to prevent the bar from rolling forward 3. Descend in a controlled manner with an upright torso until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor 4. Drive through your feet forcefully to return to the starting position

name: Goblet Squat (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Hold a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands cupping one end, feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out 2. Brace your core and keep an upright torso throughout 3. Descend in a controlled manner, pushing your knees outward in line with your toes until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor 4. Drive through your feet forcefully to return to the starting position

name: Hack Squat (Low Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet low on the platform with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out, back pressed fully against the pad 2. Release the safety handles and keep your lower back and glutes in contact with the pad throughout 3. Lower in a controlled manner until your knees reach a deep, comfortable range of motion 4. Press evenly through your feet forcefully until your legs are fully extended

name: Hack Squat (High Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet high on the platform with a shoulder-width stance and toes slightly turned out, back pressed fully against the pad 2. Release the safety handles and keep your lower back and glutes in contact with the pad throughout 3. Lower in a controlled manner until your knees reach a comfortable depth 4. Press evenly through your feet forcefully until your legs are fully extended

name: Leg Press (Plate-Loaded, Low Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet as low on the platform as possible, shoulder-width apart, without letting your heels lift significantly 2. Pull yourself down so your lower back and glutes remain pressed against the pad at all times 3. Lower the weight in a controlled manner until your knees are comfortably deep 4. Press evenly throughout your feet forcefully until your legs are fully extended

name: Leg Press (Plate-Loaded, High Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet high on the platform, shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out, lower back and glutes pressed against the pad 2. Lower the weight in a controlled manner until your knees reach a comfortable depth, keeping your lower back against the pad 3. Press evenly through your entire foot forcefully until your legs are fully extended
alias: ["high stance leg press", "leg press high stance"]

name: Leg Press (Machine, Low Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet as low on the platform as possible, shoulder-width apart, without letting your heels lift significantly 2. Keep your lower back and glutes pressed against the pad at all times 3. Lower the weight in a controlled manner until your knees are comfortably deep 4. Press evenly through your feet forcefully until your legs are fully extended

name: Leg Press (Machine, High Stance)
instruction: 1. Place your feet high on the platform, shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out, lower back and glutes pressed against the pad 2. Lower the weight in a controlled manner until your knees reach a comfortable depth 3. Press evenly through your entire foot forcefully until your legs are fully extended

name: Leg Extension (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so your knees align with the machine pivot and the shin pad rests just above your ankles, back against the pad 2. Grip the handles at your sides for stability 3. Extend your legs forcefully upward until they are fully extended 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Leg Extension (Machine, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat so your knee aligns with the machine pivot and the shin pad rests just above your ankle, back against the pad 2. Grip the handle at your side for stability 3. Extend your leg forcefully upward until it is fully extended 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Lunge (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet together, hands on hips or at your sides for balance 2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, then repeat on the other side

name: Lunge (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet together holding dumbbells at your sides 2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, then repeat on the other side

name: Lunge (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps, stand with feet together 2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, then repeat on the other side

name: Walking Lunge (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand holding dumbbells at your sides 2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee in a controlled manner toward the floor 3. Rather than returning to the start, drive through your front foot and step the rear foot forward into the next lunge 4. Continue alternating legs for the prescribed distance or reps

name: Walking Lunge (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps and stand upright 2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee in a controlled manner toward the floor 3. Drive through your front foot and step the rear foot forward into the next lunge 4. Continue alternating legs for the prescribed distance or reps

name: Reverse Lunge (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet together holding dumbbells at your sides 2. Step one foot backward and lower your rear knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, then repeat on the other side

name: Reverse Lunge (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps and stand upright 2. Step one foot backward and lower your rear knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, then repeat on the other side

name: Bulgarian Split Squat (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand a couple of feet in front of a bench, holding dumbbells at your sides, and rest the top of one foot on the bench behind you 2. Lower your rear knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your torso upright and front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, complete all reps then switch sides
alias: ["bulgarian split squat", "split squat", "rear foot elevated squat", "RFESS"]

name: Bulgarian Split Squat (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps, stand a couple of feet in front of a bench, and rest the top of one foot on the bench behind you 2. Lower your rear knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, torso upright and front shin roughly vertical 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, complete all reps then switch sides

name: Bulgarian Split Squat (Smith)
instruction: 1. Set the Smith bar across your upper traps, stand in a staggered stance with one foot forward and the other resting on a bench behind you 2. Lower your rear knee in a controlled manner toward the floor, keeping your torso upright 3. Press through your front foot forcefully to return to the starting position, complete all reps then switch sides

name: Step-Up (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Stand in front of a box or bench holding dumbbells at your sides 2. Step one foot onto the box, pressing through that heel to drive yourself up until standing fully on the box 3. Step down in a controlled manner with the trailing leg first, then repeat leading with the same leg for all reps before switching

name: Step-Up (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps and stand in front of a box or bench 2. Step one foot onto the box, pressing through that heel to drive yourself up until standing fully on the box 3. Step down in a controlled manner with the trailing leg first, complete all reps then switch lead legs

name: Sissy Squat (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip a stable support with one hand for balance 2. Rise onto your toes and lean your torso back while letting your knees travel forward over your toes 3. Lower in a controlled manner until you feel a deep stretch in your quads 4. Drive your knees back and return to the upright starting position

name: Hip Thrust (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, roll a barbell over your hips, and pad the bar for comfort 2. Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart, knees bent at roughly 90 degrees at the top 3. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line 4. Lower your hips in a controlled manner back toward the floor and repeat

name: Hip Thrust (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the machine and sit with your upper back against the pad, lap pad or belt across your hips, feet planted hip-width apart 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Hip Thrust (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Sit with your upper back against a bench, position the loaded pad across your hips, and plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully until your torso and thighs form a straight line 3. Lower in a controlled manner back toward the floor and repeat

name: Hip Thrust (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Sit with your upper back against a bench, hold a dumbbell across your hips, and plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully until your torso and thighs form a straight line 3. Lower in a controlled manner back toward the floor and repeat

name: Hip Thrust (Smith)
instruction: 1. Sit with your upper back against a bench under the Smith bar, pad the bar and position it across your hips, feet planted hip-width apart 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully until your torso and thighs form a straight line 3. Lower in a controlled manner back toward the floor and repeat

name: Glute Bridge (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on your back, roll a barbell over your hips with a pad for comfort, feet planted flat on the floor hip-width apart, knees bent 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the floor

name: Glute Bridge (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet planted flat on the floor hip-width apart 2. Drive through your heels to extend your hips forcefully upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line 3. Lower in a controlled manner back to the floor

name: Kickback (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle strap, face the cable stack and grip the frame for support 2. Brace your core and maintain a slight forward hip hinge 3. Drive the working leg backward in a controlled arc, squeezing your glute at full extension 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down, then switch sides
alias: ["glute kickback cable", "cable glute kickback", "donkey kickback cable"]

name: Kickback (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the machine, position yourself with your chest against the pad and one foot against the footpad 2. Brace your core and maintain a stable upright or slightly forward position 3. Drive your leg backward in a controlled arc, squeezing your glute at full extension 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Hip Abduction (Machine)
instruction: 1. Sit in the machine with your back against the pad, legs together, outer thighs against the pads, feet on the footrests 2. Drive your legs outward forcefully against the pads to full comfortable abduction 3. Return in a controlled manner back to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Hip Abduction (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle strap to the working leg, stand with the working leg farthest from the machine 2. Brace your core and maintain a stable upright posture, holding the frame for support 3. Drive your working leg outward and upward in a controlled arc to full comfortable abduction 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Hip Abduction (Band)
instruction: 1. Place the resistance band just above your knees and stand with feet hip-width apart, slight knee bend 2. Brace your core and maintain an upright posture 3. Drive one or both legs outward against the band to full comfortable abduction 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Seated Leg Curl (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the machine so the back of your knees align with the pivot and the shin pad rests just above your heels, back against the seat 2. Grip the handles at your sides for stability 3. Curl your legs downward forcefully until your knees are fully bent 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Lying Leg Curl (Machine)
instruction: 1. Lie face down on the machine with the back of your knees aligned with the pivot and the pad just above your heels 2. Grip the handles at the sides of the machine for stability 3. Curl your legs forcefully upward until your knees are fully bent 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down
alias: ["lying leg curl", "prone leg curl", "hamstring curl lying"]

name: Nordic Curl (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Kneel on a padded surface with your ankles secured under a stable anchor or a partner's hands, thighs vertical 2. Keeping your body in a straight line from knees to head, lower yourself forward in a controlled manner toward the floor by resisting with your hamstrings 3. Catch yourself with your hands if needed, then drive back up to the starting position

name: Glute-Ham Raise (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Position yourself on the GHD machine with your hips at the edge of the pad and feet secured under the ankle rollers 2. Start upright at the top position 3. Lower your torso forward in a controlled manner by hinging at the hips or straightening at the knees until your torso is parallel to the floor 4. Drive back up to the starting position using your hamstrings and glutes

name: Hip Adduction (Machine)
instruction: 1. Sit in the machine with legs spread wide against the outer pads, back against the seat, feet on the footrests 2. Drive your legs inward forcefully against the pads until your thighs are together or at the machine's end range 3. Return in a controlled manner back to the starting position without letting the weight stack touch down

name: Hip Adduction (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle strap to the working leg, stand with the working leg closest to the machine 2. Brace your core, hold the frame for support, and extend your working leg out to the side 3. Pull your working leg inward across your body in a controlled arc 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position, then switch sides

name: Calf Raise (Machine)
instruction: 1. Place your shoulders under the pads and the balls of your feet on the platform edge, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully, achieving full plantarflexion at the top 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Raise (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Stand with shoulders under the shoulder pads and balls of your feet on the platform edge, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Seated Calf Raise (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Sit with a loaded pad across your lower thighs just above your knees and balls of your feet on the platform edge, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Press onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Raise (Smith)
instruction: 1. Position the Smith bar across your upper traps, stand with balls of your feet on a raised platform, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the stretched starting position

name: Calf Raise (Dumbbell)
instruction: 1. Hold a dumbbell in one hand and stand with the balls of your feet on the edge of a step, heels hanging off, holding a stable surface with the other hand 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Raise (Barbell)
instruction: 1. Position the barbell across your upper traps and stand with balls of your feet on a raised platform, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Raise (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Stand with the balls of both feet on the edge of a step, heels hanging off, holding a stable surface lightly for balance 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Donkey Calf Raise (Machine)
instruction: 1. Position yourself in the machine with the pad across your lower back and balls of your feet on the platform edge, heels hanging off 2. Begin with heels lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Press (Plate-Loaded)
instruction: 1. Sit in the machine with your legs extended and the balls of your feet on the footplate, heels hanging off, knees slightly soft 2. Begin with your ankles dorsiflexed to a comfortable stretch 3. Press through the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Return in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Press (Plate-Loaded, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Sit in the machine with one leg extended and the ball of your working foot on the footplate, heel hanging off 2. Begin with your ankle dorsiflexed to a comfortable stretch 3. Press through the ball of your foot forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting stretched position, then switch sides

name: Calf Press (Machine)
instruction: 1. Sit with your legs extended and the balls of both feet on the footplate, heels hanging off 2. Begin with ankles dorsiflexed to a comfortable stretch 3. Press through the balls of your feet forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Return in a controlled manner to the starting stretched position

name: Calf Raise (Machine, Unilateral)
instruction: 1. Place one shoulder under the pad and one foot's ball on the platform edge with heel hanging off 2. Begin with your heel lowered to a comfortable stretch 3. Rise onto the ball of your foot forcefully to full plantarflexion 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting stretched position, then switch sides
alias: ["single leg calf raise", "one leg calf raise"]

name: Crunch (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hands behind your head or crossed at your chest 2. Brace your core and flex your spine to curl your shoulders off the floor toward your knees 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without fully relaxing your abs at the bottom

name: Crunch (Cable)
instruction: 1. Set the pulley to the highest position and attach a rope, kneel facing away from the stack and grip the rope ends at either side of your head 2. Brace your core and flex your spine to crunch downward toward your knees 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position

name: Crunch (Machine)
instruction: 1. Adjust the seat and grip the handles, positioning your chest against the pad or holding the handles at your head level 2. Brace your core and flex your spine to curl forward toward your knees 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without fully relaxing your abs at the bottom

name: Decline Crunch (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Secure your feet under the decline bench ankle pads and lie back, hands behind your head or crossed at your chest 2. Brace your core and flex your spine to curl your shoulders toward your knees 3. Return in a controlled manner to the starting position without fully relaxing your abs at the bottom

name: Sit-Up (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor or secured, hands behind your head or crossed at your chest 2. Brace your core and curl your torso upward until your torso is roughly upright 3. Return in a controlled manner back to the starting position

name: Leg Raise (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Lie flat on your back with legs extended, hands at your sides or under your lower back for support 2. Brace your core and keep your lower back pressed toward the floor 3. Raise both legs in a controlled manner to roughly 90 degrees while keeping them straight 4. Lower in a controlled manner back toward the floor, stopping just before your heels touch

name: Hanging Leg Raise (Bodyweight)
instruction: 1. Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms fully extended 2. Brace your core and resist swinging throughout 3. Raise your legs in a controlled manner by flexing your hips until they are roughly parallel to the floor or higher 4. Lower in a controlled manner back to the starting hang position
